narratives.evidence_quote: ऐसे में उसने पीएम मोदी का स्वागत करने के लिए उनसे वरिष्ठ पद पर बैठे राजनेता को भेजा है, जो दिखाता है कि रूस अपन संबंधों में भारत को चीन से अधिक प्राथमिकता देता है।
narratives.reasoning: The text actively praises Russia's diplomatic gesture, interpreting it as a sign of Russia valuing its relationship with India more than its relationship with China, which is a positive evaluation of Russia's foreign policy actions.

narratives.evidence_quote: भारत ने इस दौरान रूस-यूक्रेन युद्ध को लेकर अपना ये स्टैंड कायम रखा है कि केवल सैन्य माध्यमों से समाधान नहीं निकाला जा सकता। बकौल भारत, ‘ग्लोबल साउथ’ का भी मानना है कि कूटनीति व बातचीत से ही समस्या का समाधान निकलेगा, युद्धक्षेत्र में नहीं।
narratives.reasoning: The text presents India's stance that a solution cannot be found through military means alone, implying criticism of the Western-led approach which heavily supports Ukraine's military defense. It positions diplomacy and dialogue as the only solution, indirectly discrediting the Western emphasis on military support.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia has international support from a number of countries and people
subnarratives.evidence_quote: ऐसे में उसने पीएम मोदी का स्वागत करने के लिए उनसे वरिष्ठ पद पर बैठे राजनेता को भेजा है, जो दिखाता है कि रूस अपन संबंधों में भारत को चीन से अधिक प्राथमिकता देता है।
subnarratives.reasoning: The text actively promotes the idea that Russia is valued and prioritized by a major global power (India), suggesting Russia enjoys significant international support and prestige in its diplomatic relations.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: शीत युद्ध के समय से ही भारत-रूस के संबंध अच्छे हैं, साथ ही रूस भारत को सबसे अधिक हथियार सप्लाई करने वाले देशों में भी शामिल है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement praises Russia's long-standing, positive, and strategically important relationship with India, highlighting its role as a reliable and major partner. This supports the parent narrative of praising Russia but does not fit the specific definitions of military might, Putin, peace guarantor, or invasion support.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: भारत ने इस दौरान रूस-यूक्रेन युद्ध को लेकर अपना ये स्टैंड कायम रखा है कि केवल सैन्य माध्यमों से समाधान नहीं निकाला जा सकता।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly discredits the Western approach to the Ukraine war, which heavily involves military aid to Ukraine, by stating that a solution cannot be found through military means alone. This implicitly criticizes the West's strategy and promotes the idea that their current path (supporting military resistance) is ineffective, aligning with the subnarrative that diplomacy (as advocated by India and the 'Global South') is the only viable solution, thereby discrediting the West's diplomatic and strategic efforts.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: बकौल भारत, ‘ग्लोबल साउथ’ का भी मानना है कि कूटनीति व बातचीत से ही समस्या का समाधान निकलेगा, युद्धक्षेत्र में नहीं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement positions India and the 'Global South' as the proponents of the correct, peaceful diplomatic approach, in contrast to the West. It actively promotes a narrative that discredits the West's role and strategy in the Ukraine conflict by presenting an alternative, morally superior diplomatic bloc (India/Global South) that the West is presumably not heeding. This supports the parent narrative of discrediting Western diplomacy but does not fit neatly into the more specific subnarrative definitions provided (e.g., it's not about division, weakness, overreaction, etc., but about offering a contrasting, superior diplomatic vision).
